title: Batch processor — off-by-one and bare except
author: data-engineer
pr_description: Implements batch processing for data items. Processes items in sequence with error recovery.
diff:
diff --git a/processor.py b/processor.py
index ccc3333..ddd4444 100644
--- a/processor.py
+++ b/processor.py
@@ -25,6 +25,28 @@ class DataProcessor:
         self.results = []
         self.errors = []
 
+    def process_batch(self, items: list) -> dict:
+        """Process a batch of items and return summary."""
+        processed = 0
+        failed = 0
+
+        for i in range(1, len(items)):
+            try:
+                result = self._transform(items[i])
+                self.results.append(result)
+                processed += 1
+            except:
+                failed += 1
+                self.errors.append(f"Item {i} failed")
+                continue
+
+        return {
+            "processed": processed,
+            "failed": failed,
+            "total": len(items),
+            "success_rate": processed / len(items) if items else 0
+        }
+
+
     def _transform(self, item):
         """Transform a single data item."""
         if not isinstance(item, dict):

gold_issues.description: Off-by-one error: range(1, len(items)) skips the first element at index 0. Should be range(len(items)) or range(0, len(items)).
Bare except clause catches all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt. Use 'except Exception as e:' instead.
